display.purpose: Auth-screen logo title only. The one heroic moment.

headline.purpose: Screen heroes, e.g. a citizen's name on the padrón-detail screen.

title.purpose: Card titles, type-card titles, modal titles.

body.purpose: Question labels and field values. 16px floor to stop iOS focus-zoom.

label.purpose: Small bold uppercase section/field labels. Structure without weight.

shadows.name: ambient-card
shadows.purpose: Hover lift; survey cards, type cards, citizen results.

shadows.name: header-lift
shadows.purpose: Sticky gradient header with a hairline seam.

shadows.name: pop
shadows.purpose: Floating elements; toast, install card, auth card.

shadows.name: modal-weight
shadows.purpose: Blocking update modal; maximum elevation, paired with navy backdrop blur.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: The single easing curve for all state changes. No bounce.

motion.name: transition-standard
motion.purpose: Default for hover/focus/border state changes on controls.

motion.name: focus-ring
motion.purpose: Visible :focus-visible ring on every interactive control.

motion.name: spin
motion.purpose: Loading spinners and the update-modal icon (kept under reduced-motion).

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Filled blue, full-width default for the main action on a screen.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; min-height: 48px; padding: 14px 24px; border: none; border-radius: 12px; background: #0D47A1; color: #fff; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.18s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #083B87; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #1255A8; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { opacity: 0.9; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Transparent with a 2px blue border; secondary actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; min-height: 48px; padding: 14px 24px; border: 2px solid #0D47A1; border-radius: 12px; background: transparent; color: #0D47A1; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.18s ease; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: #E3F2FD; } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0D47A1; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: 16px field with hairline border; border shifts to blue on focus.
components.html: <input class="ds-input" type="text" placeholder="Buscar por DNI o apellido" />
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; border: 2px solid #DDE1E7; border-radius: 8px; padding: 12px; font-size: 16px; color: #1A1A2E; background: #fff; font-family: inherit; transition: border-color 0.18s ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #5C6677; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #0D47A1; }

components.name: Chip
components.description: Pill-shaped multi-select answer; fills blue when active.
components.css: .ds-chip { min-height: 44px; padding: 10px 16px; border: 2px solid #DDE1E7; border-radius: 20px; background: #fff; color: #1A1A2E; font-size: 0.9rem; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 0.18s ease, background 0.18s ease; } .ds-chip:hover { border-color: #0D47A1; background: #E3F2FD; } .ds-chip-active { border-color: #0D47A1; background: #0D47A1; color: #fff; } .ds-chip-active:hover { background: #0D47A1; }

components.name: Filter Tab
components.description: Lightweight pill with a count badge; used in the scrolling list filter bar.
components.css: .ds-tab { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 5px; padding: 5px 12px; border: 1.5px solid #DDE1E7; border-radius: 20px; background: transparent; color: #5C6677; font-size: 0.8rem; font-weight: 500; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; white-space: nowrap; transition: border-color 0.18s ease, color 0.18s ease; } .ds-tab:hover { border-color: #0D47A1; color: #0D47A1; } .ds-tab-active { background: #0D47A1; border-color: #0D47A1; color: #fff; } .ds-tab-count { border-radius: 10px; padding: 1px 6px; font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 700; background: #DDE1E7; color: #5C6677; } .ds-tab-count-on { background: rgba(255,255,255,.3); color: #fff; }

components.name: Survey Card
components.description: List row led by a small type-colored dot; lifts on hover.
components.html:
Problemática Centro
Falta de alumbrado público
Calle San Martín 1240
25/05/2026 · -32.97, -68.86
›
components.css: .ds-survey-card { display: flex; align-items: stretch; background: #fff; border: 1px solid #DDE1E7; border-radius: 12px; overflow: hidden; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 180ms cubic-bezier(.22,1,.36,1), box-shadow 180ms cubic-bezier(.22,1,.36,1), transform 120ms cubic-bezier(.22,1,.36,1); } .ds-survey-card:hover { border-color: #0D47A1; box-shadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(6,34,79,.10); } .ds-survey-card:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-card-accent { flex-shrink: 0; align-self: center; width: 9px; height: 9px; border-radius: 50%; margin-left: 14px; background: #F59E0B; } .ds-card-body { flex: 1; min-width: 0; padding: 12px 10px 12px 12px; } .ds-card-eyebrow { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 700; color: #5C6677; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.06em; margin-bottom: 4px; } .ds-card-barrio { font-size: 0.72rem; text-transform: none; letter-spacing: 0; background: #F4F6F9; border: 1px solid #DDE1E7; border-radius: 999px; padding: 1px 8px; color: #5C6677; } .ds-card-title { font-size: 0.97rem; font-weight: 650; color: #1A1A2E; margin-bottom: 2px; } .ds-card-sub { font-size: 0.82rem; color: #5C6677; margin-bottom: 5px; } .ds-card-meta { font-size: 0.76rem; color: #5C6677; font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace; } .ds-card-arrow { display: flex; align-items: center; padding: 0 12px 0 6px; font-size: 1.3rem; color: #C7CEDA; flex-shrink: 0; transition: color 180ms cubic-bezier(.22,1,.36,1), transform 180ms cubic-bezier(.22,1,.36,1); } .ds-survey-card:hover .ds-card-arrow { color: #0D47A1; transform: translateX(2px); }

components.name: Eyebrow Section Label
components.description: Signature small uppercase section header underlined in pale blue.
components.css: .ds-eyebrow { font-size: 0.68rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; font-weight: 700; color: #0D47A1; padding-bottom: 4px; border-bottom: 2px solid #E3F2FD; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; }

narrative.overview: Severo's identity is a constellation: deep institutional blue as the night, gold as the single bright node, and a network of households plotted across the territory of Maipú. Blue carries the surface and every primary action; gold is the rare spark reserved for the brand mark. The system reads as confident, modern, capable, a well-run operation rather than a hobby project or a legacy government form. The aesthetic is crisp and modern: flat white surfaces on a cool off-white field, separated by hairline borders rather than heavy shadows, with hierarchy from weight and uppercase eyebrow labels instead of ornament. It explicitly rejects the clunky government portal, flashy startup SaaS, the cluttered enterprise CRM, and any surveillance-tool chill.
keyCharacteristics: Deep-blue dominant surface with a single reserved gold accent.
Flat, bordered cards on a cool off-white field; shadow signals elevation or state, never sits at rest.
Native system sans for speed and familiarity; uppercase eyebrow labels for structure.
Oversized touch targets tuned for one-handed use in direct sun.
Functional color-coding: each relevamiento type owns a fixed accent hue.

rules.name: The Gold-Spark Rule
rules.body: Gold (#FFC845) belongs to the brand mark alone. Spending it on buttons or highlights destroys its meaning; emphasize with weight, size, or Constellation Blue instead.

rules.name: The Type-Hue Rule
rules.body: Each relevamiento type owns exactly one accent (ciudadano blue, problemática amber, sociohabitacional green). These hues mean record type and may not be reused decoratively.

rules.name: The Single-White Rule
rules.body: White text and icons on any colored or dark surface come from one token, --on-primary. Never type a raw #fff; if you reach for white, reach for the token.

rules.name: The 16px Floor Rule
rules.body: Any input or textarea renders at a minimum of 16px so iOS Safari does not zoom on focus and throw the volunteer out of their flow.

rules.name: The Eyebrow Rule
rules.body: Structure is signalled by small, bold, uppercase, letter-spaced labels, not by big headings.

rules.name: The Flat-At-Rest Rule
rules.body: A card sitting still has a border, not a shadow. Shadow is earned by interaction or by floating above the page.

dos: Do keep Constellation Blue (#0D47A1) as the single dominant hue: primary actions, active states, links, eyebrow titles.
Do reserve Severo Gold (#FFC845) for the brand mark only.
Do size touch targets for one hand in sunlight: 48px buttons, 44px chips, 52px radio rows, 64px scale buttons.
Do render every input at 16px minimum to prevent iOS focus-zoom.
Do signal structure with small uppercase eyebrow labels, not large headings.
Do keep surfaces flat at rest with hairline borders; let shadow mean elevation or state, and tint every shadow with the brand navy, never neutral black.
Do give every interactive control a visible :focus-visible ring; if you remove a browser outline, replace it with the soft-blue ring.
Do use the fixed type-hues (blue/amber/green) only to mean relevamiento type.
Do make every error recoverable and plainly worded; pair destructive or sensitive actions with calm confirmation.
donts: Don't build the clunky government portal: no gray dense forms, no lifeless institutional type, no bureaucratic chrome.
Don't drift toward flashy startup SaaS: no decorative gradient heroes, no emoji confetti, no marketing-app energy.
Don't crowd a screen like an enterprise CRM: one primary task per screen.
Don't let the interface feel like a surveillance or police tool; handle citizen data with visible care.
Don't spend gold on UI, add a second accent hue, or reuse a type-hue decoratively.
Don't use a colored side-stripe edge on any card, list row, or callout; encode meaning with a leading dot, label, or icon instead (the Survey Card uses a leading type dot).
Don't put a drop shadow on a static, resting panel; if it isn't elevated or interacting, it gets a border.
Don't introduce a custom display typeface; the native system stack is the deliberate choice.
